entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: Two figures reported across secondary sources without full agreement: GT (General Technical) ≥100 and CL (Clerical) ≥90 (cogn-iq.org, battalionduty.com, operationmilitarykids.org, retrieved 2026-07-12). Consistent with the Army:CL composite listed for this cluster in data/interest-taxonomy.json. UNVERIFIED at primary-source level — the official goarmy.com 42A page did not surface a numeric score requirement in this session's search-based review; confirm the current cutoff with a recruiter.
entry_requirements.education: High school diploma or GED (Army general accession standard; no 42A-specific deviation found).
entry_requirements.age_range: 17 (with parental consent) to 42, per the Army's 2026 policy ceiling (raised from 35) — data/branch-profiles.json. No 42A-specific age restriction found.
entry_requirements.physical: Standard Army accession medical and fitness standards; no elevated physical/vision standard specific to 42A was found. Must pass the Army Fitness Test (AFT) like all soldiers.
entry_requirements.security_clearance: Multiple secondary sources (usamm.com, battalionduty.com, retrieved 2026-07-12) report a Secret clearance requirement for 42A given routine access to soldiers' personnel and pay records (PII). UNVERIFIED at primary-source level this session — if accurate, this is a real qualifying requirement (background investigation) separate from and in addition to the ASVAB score; confirm with a recruiter.

training_pipeline.location: One of four Army training centers (Fort Jackson SC, Fort Moore GA, Fort Sill OK, or Fort Leonard Wood MO)
training_pipeline.description: Standard Army basic training common to all MOSs. (data/branch-profiles.json)

training_pipeline.stage: Advanced Individual Training (AIT) — Human Resources Specialist Course
training_pipeline.location: Soldier Support Institute, Fort Jackson, SC
training_pipeline.description: Personnel actions processing, military pay/records administration, and core HR competencies. Length reported as 9 weeks by most sources (operationmilitarykids.org, battalionduty.com); one source reports 8 weeks 2 days — recorded here as 9 (rounded), not independently confirmed against a primary Army source this session. One 2026-dated secondary source placed this course at Fort Gregg-Adams, VA rather than Fort Jackson — flagged as an unresolved location discrepancy, not adjudicated this session.

pay_and_compensation.methodology_note: Computed, not authoritative: (E-1, <2 YOS basic pay $2,407.20/mo + enlisted BAS $476.95/mo) x 12 = $34,609.80/yr. BAH is intentionally EXCLUDED because it is duty-station-specific and cannot be reduced to one national number (pay-tables.json bah.national_number_warning) — actual total cash compensation is materially higher once a real BAH figure for the member's duty station is added, often the single largest component. Excludes the DoD TSP contribution (up to 5% of base pay, ~$1,444/yr at max match) and TRICARE's non-cash value, neither of which pay-tables.json could quantify to primary-source confidence.
pay_and_compensation.source: data/pay-tables.json (RUN COMPENSATION, this project), citing military.com/benefits/military-pay/charts and navycs.com/charts/2026-military-pay-chart.html (primary DFAS/militarypay.defense.gov pages returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch)

bonuses.conditions: No current, verifiable enlistment or reenlistment bonus was found for any business-admin specialty in any branch this session. Administrative, HR, and finance/accounting specialties are historically LOW-bonus or NO-bonus fields across the services — they are high-accession, low-shortage, low-attrition career fields (unlike cyber, nuclear, or certain medical/aviation specialties that carry large shortage-driven bonuses), so the honest expectation for this cluster is $0 enlistment bonus in most cases. This session did not re-fetch the official HRC bonus chart (hrc.army.mil) or equivalent Navy/Air Force/Marine Corps/Coast Guard/Space Force bonus lists, consistent with this cluster's ~10-web-call budget being allocated primarily to entry requirements, training pipelines, and the credential-gap research the binding constraints prioritize. Bonuses are set quarterly per branch manning needs and change without notice — verify current bonus eligibility and amount directly with a recruiter before relying on any number.
bonuses.source: No bonus-chart source fetched this session; general bonus-structure and quarterly-change pattern corroborated by data/specialties-culinary.json's bonus research (this project), which similarly found no verifiable culinary bonus and documented the same quarterly-change caveat.

career_progression.typical_promotion_timeline: E-1→E-4 in roughly 2-3 years is the Army-wide norm; E-5 (Sergeant) board eligibility typically follows at ~4-6 years of service. No 42A-specific timeline deviation was found — treat as the general Army enlisted pattern rather than a sourced 42A-specific figure.
reenlistment_decision_points: End of initial term of service (commonly a 3- or 4-year first contract)
Mid-career (~8-10 years) reenlistment tied to promotion/NCO development school completion
The 20-year retention decision (pension cliff — see retirement block)
advanced_schools_or_certs: SHRM Certified Professional (SHRM-CP) — confirmed listed on Army COOL's credential catalog (cool.osd.mil/army/credential, found this session), funded via Army Credentialing Assistance subject to its $2,000/fiscal-year cap; NOT automatic — requires independently passing SHRM's exam

Professional in Human Resources (PHR) — HRCI credential, also confirmed COOL-eligible via Navy/Coast Guard COOL pages found this session; the specific Army 42A-to-PHR COOL linkage was not independently confirmed this session
Basic/Advanced NCO courses at each promotion requirement (standard Army NCOES, not 42A-specific)
career_progression.officer_conversion_path: Green-to-Gold (enlisted-to-officer commissioning via Army ROTC) is a general Army pathway open to qualifying soldiers of any MOS, including 42A. No 42A-specific guaranteed-branch officer conversion program (e.g., a direct-commission Adjutant General Corps track tied specifically to this MOS) was identified this session.
career_progression.source: cool.osd.mil/army/credential (SHRM-CP/PHR listings, found via search this session); https://www.usamm.com/blogs/news/42a-mos-human-resources-specialist

retirement.twenty_year_cliff_note: The pension is all-or-nothing at 20 years — there is no partial/prorated defined-benefit pension for separating at 19 years or 10 years. Before 20 years, a member in this specialty keeps every dollar of their own TSP contributions and earnings (always vested), plus DoD's automatic 1%-of-pay TSP contribution and its match (vested after 2 years of service) — all portable into a civilian 401(k)/IRA. The defined-benefit pension itself is 'entirely forfeited' below 20 years, per DoD's own language. This applies identically regardless of specialty; business-admin carries no special rule.
retirement.continuation_pay_note: One-time cash payout available between roughly year 7 and year 12 of service (exact window and multiplier set annually per branch; Army's published active-component rate is 2.5x monthly basic pay for a 4-year additional service obligation). No business-admin-specific continuation-pay rate was identified this session — treat the general BRS figure as the baseline and verify current-branch amount before relying on it.
retirement.guard_reserve_points_note: Guard/Reserve retirement is NOT interchangeable with active-duty retirement. It runs on a points system (1 point/drill, 1 point/AD day, up to 15 membership points/year) toward a 20-'good year' qualifying threshold, with a non-regular pension that generally starts at age 60 (reducible, not below age 50, for qualifying post-2008 mobilized duty). Applies identically to Guard/Reserve business-admin billets as to any other specialty.
retirement.lump_sum_option_note: At 20+ years, a retiring member may elect 25% or 50% of the discounted present value of future retired pay as an immediate lump sum, in exchange for reduced monthly retired pay until full Social Security retirement age (~67), when the monthly amount returns to the uncut level. Fully taxable in the year received.

transition.skillbridge_notes: DoD-wide SkillBridge eligibility (up to 180 days of permissive duty in the final window before separation) is not specialty-restricted, so business-admin service members near separation are as eligible as any other MOS/rating/AFSC in principle — subject to the same real requirement as everyone else: unit commander approval, which is discretionary based on unit staffing/operational needs (data/transition.json). Administrative and finance skill sets are commonly matched with corporate HR-department, staffing-agency, or finance/accounting-firm SkillBridge industry partners in general SkillBridge program materials, but whether such partners are available in a given service member's location was not independently verified this session — flagged as an inference, not a sourced claim.
transition.tap_program_summary: Mandatory DoD-wide Transition Assistance Program: pre-separation counseling, a VA Benefits I course, a DOL employment-fundamentals workshop, and an optional 2-day track (Higher Education, Career/Technical Training, or Entrepreneurship/Boots to Business), ending in a Capstone that certifies Career Readiness Standards are met. Must begin no later than 365 days before separation. Applies identically regardless of specialty.
transition.dd214_significance: The DD-214's 'character of service' entry decides eligibility for nearly every benefit below — VA disability, VA healthcare, GI Bill, VA home loan, and most state veteran benefits. No business-admin-specific significance beyond the general rule.
va_disability_common_claims: GUESS/INFERENCE, not sourced from a specialty-linked VA claims dataset this session (data/transition.json's specialty_linked_common_claims list covers combat arms, aviation, infantry, and PACT Act-covered theater deployment, but does NOT include an administrative/HR/finance entry — this is a desk-bound career field with a structurally different injury/exposure profile than the combat, aviation, and infantry claims that dominate that dataset). Reasoned by analogy to general office/administrative-work injury and mental-health-claim patterns, not pulled from a VA claims-frequency source:
Musculoskeletal conditions (cervical/lumbar spine strain, carpal tunnel syndrome) from years of prolonged desk/computer work processing records and pay actions

Mental health conditions (anxiety, depression, adjustment disorder) — plausible given the emotionally demanding nature of administrative duties that intersect with casualty notification support, separations/discharge processing, and military-justice/legal-administrative caseloads, though this is a reasoned inference, not a sourced claims-frequency figure

Hearing loss/tinnitus is LESS characteristic of this cluster than of combat arms or aviation — administrative/finance roles are predominantly office-based; any hearing-related claim would more plausibly stem from a service member's earlier or concurrent field/range/flightline exposure than from the administrative duties themselves

To verify: check VA's published claims-by-occupation or claims-by-MOS data (if VA publishes it) or consult a VSO familiar with administrative/finance-career veterans before treating this list as authoritative. Business-admin specialties are NOT among the primary drivers of VA disability claims relative to combat arms, aviation, or PACT Act-covered deployments — this honest baseline should not be inflated to match higher-claim specialties.
transition.vr_e_eligibility_note: Requires a 20%+ service-connected disability rating creating an employment handicap, filed within a 12-year window from separation or first rating. Funds up to 48 months of training/education plus a monthly subsistence allowance (FY2026 max ~$3,439.23/mo per data/transition.json), drawn from a separate entitlement bucket than the Post-9/11 GI Bill. No business-admin-specific eligibility variance.

onet_codes: 43-4051.00 — Human Resources Assistants, Except Payroll and Timekeeping
relevant_certifications: SHRM Certified Professional (SHRM-CP) — Society for Human Resource Management; requires passing SHRM's exam, not automatic from military service
Professional in Human Resources (PHR) — HR Certification Institute (HRCI); requires passing HRCI's exam plus a minimum experience/education threshold, not automatic from military service
civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: This is the most consequential honest fact about this cluster's HR-facing specialties, per this project's binding constraint: military personnel/HR-adjacent experience transfers reasonably well as EXPERIENCE — a hiring manager will credit years of processing personnel actions, records, and pay support — but it confers NO automatic civilian HR certification. Neither SHRM-CP nor PHR is awarded for military service; both require independently registering for and passing a proctored exam (SHRM-CP also expects a baseline of HR knowledge; PHR/HRCI credentials have their own experience/education eligibility criteria). The honest bridge is COOL / Credentialing Assistance funding the exam and prep costs while still serving, not a transcript-based automatic award — see education_benefits.credentialing_assistance below and data/transition.json's credential_gap section for the same principle applied to other specialties (e.g., 68W→EMT, 31B→POST).
civilian_crosswalk.source: BLS OEWS, Human Resources Specialists (SOC 13-1071), median $67,650/yr, current-year estimates reported via web search synthesis of bls.gov/oes data this session — not independently WebFetched to a specific dated OEWS release page; treat the range as directional, not quote-verified. SHRM/HRCI credential descriptions from shrm.org/credentials and cool.osd.mil SHRM-CP/PHR credential pages found via search this session.

veteran_benefits.gi_bill_tier_note: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33) benefit tier scales with cumulative qualifying active-duty days: 100% at 36+ months (or Purple Heart / service-connected-disability discharge after 30+ days), down through 90%, 80%, 70%, 60%, to a 50% floor at 90 days-5 months. No tier below 50%. Only qualifying Title 10 active-duty days count — drills, AT, and initial training duty status do not. Applies identically regardless of specialty.
veteran_benefits.gi_bill_transferability_note: Transferable to a spouse and/or dependent children (up to 36 months combined) after 6 years of service, in exchange for a 4-year additional service-obligation commitment — a real service commitment, not paperwork-only. Must be requested via milConnect while still serving; cannot be initiated after separation. Dependent children must wait until the member has 10+ years of service to start using it.
veteran_benefits.va_home_loan_note: VA-backed home loans generally require no down payment and no monthly PMI for veterans with full entitlement, subject to lender underwriting (credit/income/DTI) and a funding fee (1.25%-3.3% of loan amount depending on down payment and first/subsequent use, waived for veterans drawing service-connected disability compensation).
veteran_benefits.va_healthcare_priority_group_note: VA assigns one of 8 priority groups at enrollment based on service-connected disability rating, combat/toxic-exposure service history, income, and other VA-benefit receipt — not by specialty. A business-admin veteran's group assignment depends entirely on their individual disability rating and income profile, not their MOS/rating/AFSC.
veteran_benefits.state_benefit_variance_note: State veteran benefits (property-tax exemptions, income-tax treatment, tuition waivers, etc.) are set by individual state legislatures and are NOT a single national figure — e.g., a 100%-disabled veteran gets a full residence-homestead property-tax exemption in Texas but a flat $100,000 assessed-value exemption in California (data/veteran-benefits.json). Verify against the veteran's specific state of legal residence.

education_benefits.gi_bill_summary: Post-9/11 GI Bill pays full tuition/fees at public schools (in-state rate applies to all users) or up to $29,920.95/yr (2025-2026 AY) at private/foreign schools, plus a housing allowance keyed to the school's zip code and a $1,000/yr book stipend — scaled by the eligibility tier above. (data/education-benefits.json)
education_benefits.tuition_assistance: DoD-wide Tuition Assistance: $250/semester-hour, capped at $4,500/fiscal year, while still serving. Applies the same across all six branches per data/education-benefits.json; branch-specific semester-hour usage limits were not independently confirmed to primary-source level in that file.
education_benefits.credentialing_assistance: Army Credentialing Assistance (CA)/Army COOL: reduced to a $2,000/fiscal-year cap in 2024, lifetime limit reported at 3 credentials per 10 years of service; commissioned officers reported ineligible. UNVERIFIED at full primary-source level — cool.osd.mil's homepage and credential-listing pages were reachable via search this session, corroborating SHRM-CP/PHR listings, but the $2,000 cap figure itself traces to data/education-benefits.json's prior research (stripes.com, myarmybenefits.us.army.mil), not a fresh fetch this session.
education_benefits.coop_or_apprenticeship_notes: SHRM-CP and PHR are the relevant civilian HR-credential pathway (see civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note) — NOT automatic from military service. Army COOL lists both as fundable credentials for HR-adjacent career fields; a soldier would apply Army CA funding toward exam and prep costs while still serving, then sit the independent SHRM or HRCI exam. (cool.osd.mil, found via search this session.)

deployment_and_lifestyle.typical_tempo: Garrison-centered administrative role; deploys with the supported unit's S-1 (personnel) shop during deployments to process personnel actions, casualty reporting, and pay actions in theater. No sourced 42A-specific deployment-frequency figure was found this session; tempo generally follows the supported unit's operational tempo rather than a MOS-specific rotation.
deployment_and_lifestyle.family_considerations: Relatively family-stable compared to combat-arms and sea-service specialties given the garrison/desk-based nature of the work; deployments follow the supported unit's schedule, which varies widely by unit type.

relevant_certifications: Certified Bookkeeper (CB) — American Institute of Professional Bookkeepers (AIPB) or National Association of Certified Public Bookkeepers (NACPB); requires passing an independent exam, not automatic from military service

civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: Per this project's binding constraint on this cluster: military financial-management/accounting-technician experience does NOT confer a CPA license or an accounting degree. State CPA boards require a bachelor's degree with 150 semester hours of specific accounting/business coursework (30 hours beyond a typical 120-hour bachelor's) and a passing score on the Uniform CPA Examination — military finance-technician training and on-the-job experience do not substitute for the coursework requirement or waive the exam. The honest, achievable near-term bridge is a civilian bookkeeping certification (CB) or, for those pursuing federal/government finance careers specifically, the CGFM — both still require passing an independent exam, funded (not automatically awarded) via COOL/Credentialing Assistance while still serving.

veteran_benefits.state_benefit_variance_note: State veteran benefits (property-tax exemptions, income-tax treatment, tuition waivers, etc.) are set by individual state legislatures and are NOT a single national figure — e.g., a 100%-disabled veteran gets a full residence-homestead property-tax exemption in Texas but a flat $100,000 assessed-value exemption in California (data/veteran-benefits.json). Verify against the veteran's specific state of legal residence.

education_benefits.gi_bill_summary: Post-9/11 GI Bill pays full tuition/fees at public schools (in-state rate applies to all users) or up to $29,920.95/yr (2025-2026 AY) at private/foreign schools, plus a housing allowance keyed to the school's zip code and a $1,000/yr book stipend — scaled by the eligibility tier above. (data/education-benefits.json)
education_benefits.tuition_assistance: DoD-wide Tuition Assistance: $250/semester-hour, capped at $4,500/fiscal year, while still serving. Applies the same across all six branches per data/education-benefits.json; branch-specific semester-hour usage limits were not independently confirmed to primary-source level in that file.
education_benefits.credentialing_assistance: Army Credentialing Assistance (CA)/Army COOL: reduced to a $2,000/fiscal-year cap in 2024, lifetime limit reported at 3 credentials per 10 years of service; commissioned officers reported ineligible. UNVERIFIED at full primary-source level — inherited from data/education-benefits.json, not independently re-fetched this session.
education_benefits.coop_or_apprenticeship_notes: CGFM and civilian bookkeeping certifications are the relevant credential pathway (see civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note) — NOT automatic from military service. Army CA funding would apply toward exam/prep costs for a soldier pursuing these while still serving; no primary Army COOL page specifically confirming 36B-to-CGFM eligibility was fetched this session.

deployment_and_lifestyle.typical_tempo: Garrison-centered administrative/finance role; deploys with the supported unit's finance detachment or S-1/S-4 shop during deployments to process pay, travel, and contracting-support financial actions in theater. No sourced 36B-specific deployment-frequency figure was found this session; tempo generally follows the supported unit's operational tempo.
deployment_and_lifestyle.family_considerations: Relatively family-stable compared to combat-arms and sea-service specialties given the garrison/desk-based nature of the work; deployments follow the supported unit's schedule, which varies widely by unit type.

bonuses.conditions: No current, verifiable enlistment or reenlistment bonus was found for any business-admin specialty in any branch this session. Administrative, HR, and finance/accounting specialties are historically LOW-bonus or NO-bonus fields across the services — they are high-accession, low-shortage, low-attrition career fields (unlike cyber, nuclear, or certain medical/aviation specialties that carry large shortage-driven bonuses), so the honest expectation for this cluster is $0 enlistment bonus in most cases. This session did not re-fetch the official HRC bonus chart (hrc.army.mil) or equivalent Navy/Air Force/Marine Corps/Coast Guard/Space Force bonus lists, consistent with this cluster's ~10-web-call budget being allocated primarily to entry requirements, training pipelines, and the credential-gap research the binding constraints prioritize. Bonuses are set quarterly per branch manning needs and change without notice — verify current bonus eligibility and amount directly with a recruiter before relying on any number.
bonuses.source: No bonus-chart source fetched this session; general bonus-structure and quarterly-change pattern corroborated by data/specialties-culinary.json's bonus research (this project), which similarly found no verifiable culinary bonus and documented the same quarterly-change caveat.

career_progression.typical_promotion_timeline: E-1→E-4 in roughly 2-3 years and E-5 board eligibility at ~4-6 years is the Navy-wide enlisted norm; no YN-specific timeline deviation was found this session — general pattern, not a sourced YN-specific figure.
reenlistment_decision_points: End of initial enlistment contract (commonly 4 or 6 years)
Rating conversion to Legalman (LN) for those pursuing the legal/paralegal track, or selection for specialized YN duty (flag writer, career counselor, legal-specialty training)
The 20-year retention decision (pension cliff — see retirement block)
advanced_schools_or_certs: SHRM Certified Professional (SHRM-CP) and Professional in Human Resources (PHR) — both confirmed listed on Navy COOL's credential catalog (cool.osd.mil/usn/credential, found this session), funded subject to Navy COOL's credentialing-assistance program (specific dollar cap UNVERIFIED per data/education-benefits.json)
Navy Enlisted Classification (NEC) codes for specialized YN duty (e.g., legal, career counselor, flag writer)
career_progression.officer_conversion_path: Limited Duty Officer (LDO) and Chief Warrant Officer (CWO) programs are general Navy enlisted-to-officer pathways open to senior YNs with qualifying experience; no YN-specific guaranteed program was identified this session.

retirement.twenty_year_cliff_note: The pension is all-or-nothing at 20 years — there is no partial/prorated defined-benefit pension for separating at 19 years or 10 years. Before 20 years, a member in this specialty keeps every dollar of their own TSP contributions and earnings (always vested), plus DoD's automatic 1%-of-pay TSP contribution and its match (vested after 2 years of service) — all portable into a civilian 401(k)/IRA. The defined-benefit pension itself is 'entirely forfeited' below 20 years, per DoD's own language. This applies identically regardless of specialty; business-admin carries no special rule.
retirement.continuation_pay_note: One-time cash payout available between roughly year 7 and year 12 of service (exact window and multiplier set annually per branch; Army's published active-component rate is 2.5x monthly basic pay for a 4-year additional service obligation). No business-admin-specific continuation-pay rate was identified this session — treat the general BRS figure as the baseline and verify current-branch amount before relying on it.
retirement.guard_reserve_points_note: Guard/Reserve retirement is NOT interchangeable with active-duty retirement. It runs on a points system (1 point/drill, 1 point/AD day, up to 15 membership points/year) toward a 20-'good year' qualifying threshold, with a non-regular pension that generally starts at age 60 (reducible, not below age 50, for qualifying post-2008 mobilized duty). Applies identically to Guard/Reserve business-admin billets as to any other specialty.
retirement.lump_sum_option_note: At 20+ years, a retiring member may elect 25% or 50% of the discounted present value of future retired pay as an immediate lump sum, in exchange for reduced monthly retired pay until full Social Security retirement age (~67), when the monthly amount returns to the uncut level. Fully taxable in the year received.

transition.skillbridge_notes: DoD-wide SkillBridge eligibility (up to 180 days of permissive duty in the final window before separation) is not specialty-restricted, so business-admin service members near separation are as eligible as any other MOS/rating/AFSC in principle — subject to the same real requirement as everyone else: unit commander approval, which is discretionary based on unit staffing/operational needs (data/transition.json). Administrative and finance skill sets are commonly matched with corporate HR-department, staffing-agency, or finance/accounting-firm SkillBridge industry partners in general SkillBridge program materials, but whether such partners are available in a given service member's location was not independently verified this session — flagged as an inference, not a sourced claim.
transition.tap_program_summary: Mandatory DoD-wide Transition Assistance Program: pre-separation counseling, a VA Benefits I course, a DOL employment-fundamentals workshop, and an optional 2-day track (Higher Education, Career/Technical Training, or Entrepreneurship/Boots to Business), ending in a Capstone that certifies Career Readiness Standards are met. Must begin no later than 365 days before separation. Applies identically regardless of specialty.
transition.dd214_significance: The DD-214's 'character of service' entry decides eligibility for nearly every benefit below — VA disability, VA healthcare, GI Bill, VA home loan, and most state veteran benefits. No business-admin-specific significance beyond the general rule.
va_disability_common_claims: GUESS/INFERENCE, not sourced from a specialty-linked VA claims dataset this session (data/transition.json's specialty_linked_common_claims list covers combat arms, aviation, infantry, and PACT Act-covered theater deployment, but does NOT include an administrative/HR/finance entry — this is a desk-bound career field with a structurally different injury/exposure profile than the combat, aviation, and infantry claims that dominate that dataset). Reasoned by analogy to general office/administrative-work injury and mental-health-claim patterns, not pulled from a VA claims-frequency source:
Musculoskeletal conditions (cervical/lumbar spine strain, carpal tunnel syndrome) from years of prolonged desk/computer work processing records and pay actions

Mental health conditions (anxiety, depression, adjustment disorder) — plausible given the emotionally demanding nature of administrative duties that intersect with casualty notification support, separations/discharge processing, and military-justice/legal-administrative caseloads, though this is a reasoned inference, not a sourced claims-frequency figure

Hearing loss/tinnitus is LESS characteristic of this cluster than of combat arms or aviation — administrative/finance roles are predominantly office-based; any hearing-related claim would more plausibly stem from a service member's earlier or concurrent field/range/flightline exposure than from the administrative duties themselves

To verify: check VA's published claims-by-occupation or claims-by-MOS data (if VA publishes it) or consult a VSO familiar with administrative/finance-career veterans before treating this list as authoritative. Business-admin specialties are NOT among the primary drivers of VA disability claims relative to combat arms, aviation, or PACT Act-covered deployments — this honest baseline should not be inflated to match higher-claim specialties.
transition.vr_e_eligibility_note: Requires a 20%+ service-connected disability rating creating an employment handicap, filed within a 12-year window from separation or first rating. Funds up to 48 months of training/education plus a monthly subsistence allowance (FY2026 max ~$3,439.23/mo per data/transition.json), drawn from a separate entitlement bucket than the Post-9/11 GI Bill. No business-admin-specific eligibility variance.

relevant_certifications: SHRM Certified Professional (SHRM-CP) — Society for Human Resource Management; requires passing SHRM's exam, not automatic from military service
Professional in Human Resources (PHR) — HR Certification Institute (HRCI); requires passing HRCI's exam plus a minimum experience/education threshold, not automatic from military service
civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: This is the most consequential honest fact about this cluster's HR-facing specialties, per this project's binding constraint: military personnel/HR-adjacent experience transfers reasonably well as EXPERIENCE — a hiring manager will credit years of processing personnel actions, records, and pay support — but it confers NO automatic civilian HR certification. Neither SHRM-CP nor PHR is awarded for military service; both require independently registering for and passing a proctored exam (SHRM-CP also expects a baseline of HR knowledge; PHR/HRCI credentials have their own experience/education eligibility criteria). The honest bridge is COOL / Credentialing Assistance funding the exam and prep costs while still serving, not a transcript-based automatic award — see education_benefits.credentialing_assistance below and data/transition.json's credential_gap section for the same principle applied to other specialties (e.g., 68W→EMT, 31B→POST).
civilian_crosswalk.source: BLS OEWS, Human Resources Specialists (SOC 13-1071), median $67,650/yr, current-year estimates reported via web search synthesis of bls.gov/oes data this session — not independently WebFetched to a specific dated OEWS release page; treat the range as directional, not quote-verified. SHRM/HRCI credential descriptions from shrm.org/credentials and cool.osd.mil SHRM-CP/PHR credential pages found via search this session.

veteran_benefits.gi_bill_tier_note: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33) benefit tier scales with cumulative qualifying active-duty days: 100% at 36+ months (or Purple Heart / service-connected-disability discharge after 30+ days), down through 90%, 80%, 70%, 60%, to a 50% floor at 90 days-5 months. No tier below 50%. Only qualifying Title 10 active-duty days count — drills, AT, and initial training duty status do not. Applies identically regardless of specialty.
veteran_benefits.gi_bill_transferability_note: Transferable to a spouse and/or dependent children (up to 36 months combined) after 6 years of service, in exchange for a 4-year additional service-obligation commitment — a real service commitment, not paperwork-only. Must be requested via milConnect while still serving; cannot be initiated after separation. Dependent children must wait until the member has 10+ years of service to start using it.
veteran_benefits.va_home_loan_note: VA-backed home loans generally require no down payment and no monthly PMI for veterans with full entitlement, subject to lender underwriting (credit/income/DTI) and a funding fee (1.25%-3.3% of loan amount depending on down payment and first/subsequent use, waived for veterans drawing service-connected disability compensation).
veteran_benefits.va_healthcare_priority_group_note: VA assigns one of 8 priority groups at enrollment based on service-connected disability rating, combat/toxic-exposure service history, income, and other VA-benefit receipt — not by specialty. A business-admin veteran's group assignment depends entirely on their individual disability rating and income profile, not their MOS/rating/AFSC.

(data/education-benefits.json)
education_benefits.tuition_assistance: DoD-wide Tuition Assistance: $250/semester-hour, capped at $4,500/fiscal year, while still serving. Applies the same across all six branches per data/education-benefits.json; branch-specific semester-hour usage limits were not independently confirmed to primary-source level in that file.
education_benefits.credentialing_assistance: Navy Credentialing Opportunities On-Line (COOL) funds credential exams/renewals/fees for active-duty Navy and Navy Reserve (excluding IRR/VTU); a specific dollar cap was NOT confirmed this session (cool.osd.mil fetch attempts in prior project sessions failed with a certificate error). (data/education-benefits.json)
education_benefits.coop_or_apprenticeship_notes: SHRM-CP and PHR are confirmed listed on Navy COOL (cool.osd.mil/usn/credential, found via search this session) as fundable HR-adjacent credentials for YN — NOT automatic from military service; a sailor would apply COOL funding toward exam/prep costs while still serving, then sit the independent SHRM or HRCI exam.

deployment_and_lifestyle.family_considerations: Sea-duty tours involve the standard Navy underway/deployment separation pattern common to all shipboard ratings, alternating with shore-duty tours that are more family-stable.

veteran_benefits.gi_bill_tier_note: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33) benefit tier scales with cumulative qualifying active-duty days: 100% at 36+ months (or Purple Heart / service-connected-disability discharge after 30+ days), down through 90%, 80%, 70%, 60%, to a 50% floor at 90 days-5 months. No tier below 50%. Only qualifying Title 10 active-duty days count — drills, AT, and initial training duty status do not. Applies identically regardless of specialty.
veteran_benefits.gi_bill_transferability_note: Transferable to a spouse and/or dependent children (up to 36 months combined) after 6 years of service, in exchange for a 4-year additional service-obligation commitment — a real service commitment, not paperwork-only. Must be requested via milConnect while still serving; cannot be initiated after separation. Dependent children must wait until the member has 10+ years of service to start using it.
veteran_benefits.va_home_loan_note: VA-backed home loans generally require no down payment and no monthly PMI for veterans with full entitlement, subject to lender underwriting (credit/income/DTI) and a funding fee (1.25%-3.3% of loan amount depending on down payment and first/subsequent use, waived for veterans drawing service-connected disability compensation).
veteran_benefits.va_healthcare_priority_group_note: VA assigns one of 8 priority groups at enrollment based on service-connected disability rating, combat/toxic-exposure service history, income, and other VA-benefit receipt — not by specialty. A business-admin veteran's group assignment depends entirely on their individual disability rating and income profile, not their MOS/rating/AFSC.
veteran_benefits.state_benefit_variance_note: State veteran benefits (property-tax exemptions, income-tax treatment, tuition waivers, etc.) are set by individual state legislatures and are NOT a single national figure — e.g., a 100%-disabled veteran gets a full residence-homestead property-tax exemption in Texas but a flat $100,000 assessed-value exemption in California (data/veteran-benefits.json). Verify against the veteran's specific state of legal residence.

(data/education-benefits.json)

bonuses.conditions: No current, verifiable enlistment or reenlistment bonus was found for any business-admin specialty in any branch this session. Administrative, HR, and finance/accounting specialties are historically LOW-bonus or NO-bonus fields across the services — they are high-accession, low-shortage, low-attrition career fields (unlike cyber, nuclear, or certain medical/aviation specialties that carry large shortage-driven bonuses), so the honest expectation for this cluster is $0 enlistment bonus in most cases. This session did not re-fetch the official HRC bonus chart (hrc.army.mil) or equivalent Navy/Air Force/Marine Corps/Coast Guard/Space Force bonus lists, consistent with this cluster's ~10-web-call budget being allocated primarily to entry requirements, training pipelines, and the credential-gap research the binding constraints prioritize. retirement.twenty_year_cliff_note: The pension is all-or-nothing at 20 years — there is no partial/prorated defined-benefit pension for separating at 19 years or 10 years. Before 20 years, a member in this specialty keeps every dollar of their own TSP contributions and earnings (always vested), plus DoD's automatic 1%-of-pay TSP contribution and its match (vested after 2 years of service) — all portable into a civilian 401(k)/IRA. The defined-benefit pension itself is 'entirely forfeited' below 20 years, per DoD's own language. This applies identically regardless of specialty; business-admin carries no special rule.
retirement.continuation_pay_note: One-time cash payout available between roughly year 7 and year 12 of service (exact window and multiplier set annually per branch; Army's published active-component rate is 2.5x monthly basic pay for a 4-year additional service obligation). No business-admin-specific continuation-pay rate was identified this session — treat the general BRS figure as the baseline and verify current-branch amount before relying on it.
retirement.guard_reserve_points_note: Guard/Reserve retirement is NOT interchangeable with active-duty retirement. It runs on a points system (1 point/drill, 1 point/AD day, up to 15 membership points/year) toward a 20-'good year' qualifying threshold, with a non-regular pension that generally starts at age 60 (reducible, not below age 50, for qualifying post-2008 mobilized duty). Applies identically to Guard/Reserve business-admin billets as to any other specialty.
retirement.lump_sum_option_note: At 20+ years, a retiring member may elect 25% or 50% of the discounted present value of future retired pay as an immediate lump sum, in exchange for reduced monthly retired pay until full Social Security retirement age (~67), when the monthly amount returns to the uncut level. Fully taxable in the year received.

transition.skillbridge_notes: DoD-wide SkillBridge eligibility (up to 180 days of permissive duty in the final window before separation) is not specialty-restricted, so business-admin service members near separation are as eligible as any other MOS/rating/AFSC in principle — subject to the same real requirement as everyone else: unit commander approval, which is discretionary based on unit staffing/operational needs (data/transition.json). Administrative and finance skill sets are commonly matched with corporate HR-department, staffing-agency, or finance/accounting-firm SkillBridge industry partners in general SkillBridge program materials, but whether such partners are available in a given service member's location was not independently verified this session — flagged as an inference, not a sourced claim.
transition.tap_program_summary: Mandatory DoD-wide Transition Assistance Program: pre-separation counseling, a VA Benefits I course, a DOL employment-fundamentals workshop, and an optional 2-day track (Higher Education, Career/Technical Training, or Entrepreneurship/Boots to Business), ending in a Capstone that certifies Career Readiness Standards are met. Must begin no later than 365 days before separation. Applies identically regardless of specialty.
transition.dd214_significance: The DD-214's 'character of service' entry decides eligibility for nearly every benefit below — VA disability, VA healthcare, GI Bill, VA home loan, and most state veteran benefits. No business-admin-specific significance beyond the general rule.
va_disability_common_claims: GUESS/INFERENCE, not sourced from a specialty-linked VA claims dataset this session (data/transition.json's specialty_linked_common_claims list covers combat arms, aviation, infantry, and PACT Act-covered theater deployment, but does NOT include an administrative/HR/finance entry — this is a desk-bound career field with a structurally different injury/exposure profile than the combat, aviation, and infantry claims that dominate that dataset). Reasoned by analogy to general office/administrative-work injury and mental-health-claim patterns, not pulled from a VA claims-frequency source:
Musculoskeletal conditions (cervical/lumbar spine strain, carpal tunnel syndrome) from years of prolonged desk/computer work processing records and pay actions

Mental health conditions (anxiety, depression, adjustment disorder) — plausible given the emotionally demanding nature of administrative duties that intersect with casualty notification support, separations/discharge processing, and military-justice/legal-administrative caseloads, though this is a reasoned inference, not a sourced claims-frequency figure

Hearing loss/tinnitus is LESS characteristic of this cluster than of combat arms or aviation — administrative/finance roles are predominantly office-based; any hearing-related claim would more plausibly stem from a service member's earlier or concurrent field/range/flightline exposure than from the administrative duties themselves

To verify: check VA's published claims-by-occupation or claims-by-MOS data (if VA publishes it) or consult a VSO familiar with administrative/finance-career veterans before treating this list as authoritative. Business-admin specialties are NOT among the primary drivers of VA disability claims relative to combat arms, aviation, or PACT Act-covered deployments — this honest baseline should not be inflated to match higher-claim specialties.
transition.vr_e_eligibility_note: Requires a 20%+ service-connected disability rating creating an employment handicap, filed within a 12-year window from separation or first rating. Funds up to 48 months of training/education plus a monthly subsistence allowance (FY2026 max ~$3,439.23/mo per data/transition.json), drawn from a separate entitlement bucket than the Post-9/11 GI Bill. No business-admin-specific eligibility variance.

Reasoned by analogy to general office/administrative-work injury and mental-health-claim patterns, not pulled from a VA claims-frequency source:
Musculoskeletal conditions (cervical/lumbar spine strain, carpal tunnel syndrome) from years of prolonged desk/computer work processing records and pay actions

Mental health conditions (anxiety, depression, adjustment disorder) — plausible given the emotionally demanding nature of administrative duties that intersect with casualty notification support, separations/discharge processing, and military-justice/legal-administrative caseloads, though this is a reasoned inference, not a sourced claims-frequency figure

Hearing loss/tinnitus is LESS characteristic of this cluster than of combat arms or aviation — administrative/finance roles are predominantly office-based; any hearing-related claim would more plausibly stem from a service member's earlier or concurrent field/range/flightline exposure than from the administrative duties themselves

To verify: check VA's published claims-by-occupation or claims-by-MOS data (if VA publishes it) or consult a VSO familiar with administrative/finance-career veterans before treating this list as authoritative. Business-admin specialties are NOT among the primary drivers of VA disability claims relative to combat arms, aviation, or PACT Act-covered deployments — this honest baseline should not be inflated to match higher-claim specialties.
transition.vr_e_eligibility_note: Requires a 20%+ service-connected disability rating creating an employment handicap, filed within a 12-year window from separation or first rating. Funds up to 48 months of training/education plus a monthly subsistence allowance (FY2026 max ~$3,439.23/mo per data/transition.json), drawn from a separate entitlement bucket than the Post-9/11 GI Bill. No business-admin-specific eligibility variance.

onet_codes: 43-4051.00 — Human Resources Assistants, Except Payroll and Timekeeping
relevant_certifications: SHRM Certified Professional (SHRM-CP) — Society for Human Resource Management; requires passing SHRM's exam, not automatic from military service
Professional in Human Resources (PHR) — HR Certification Institute (HRCI); requires passing HRCI's exam plus a minimum experience/education threshold, not automatic from military service
civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: This is the most consequential honest fact about this cluster's HR-facing specialties, per this project's binding constraint: military personnel/HR-adjacent experience transfers reasonably well as EXPERIENCE — a hiring manager will credit years of processing personnel actions, records, and pay support — but it confers NO automatic civilian HR certification. Neither SHRM-CP nor PHR is awarded for military service; both require independently registering for and passing a proctored exam (SHRM-CP also expects a baseline of HR knowledge; PHR/HRCI credentials have their own experience/education eligibility criteria). The honest bridge is COOL / Credentialing Assistance funding the exam and prep costs while still serving, not a transcript-based automatic award — see education_benefits.credentialing_assistance below and data/transition.json's credential_gap section for the same principle applied to other specialties (e.g., 68W→EMT, 31B→POST).
civilian_crosswalk.source: BLS OEWS, Human Resources Specialists (SOC 13-1071), median $67,650/yr, current-year estimates reported via web search synthesis of bls.gov/oes data this session — not independently WebFetched to a specific dated OEWS release page; treat the range as directional, not quote-verified. SHRM/HRCI credential descriptions from shrm.org/credentials and cool.osd.mil SHRM-CP/PHR credential pages found via search this session.

veteran_benefits.gi_bill_tier_note: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch. 33) benefit tier scales with cumulative qualifying active-duty days: 100% at 36+ months (or Purple Heart / service-connected-disability discharge after 30+ days), down through 90%, 80%, 70%, 60%, to a 50% floor at 90 days-5 months. No tier below 50%. Only qualifying Title 10 active-duty days count — drills, AT, and initial training duty status do not. Applies identically regardless of specialty.
veteran_benefits.gi_bill_transferability_note: Transferable to a spouse and/or dependent children (up to 36 months combined) after 6 years of service, in exchange for a 4-year additional service-obligation commitment — a real service commitment, not paperwork-only. Must be requested via milConnect while still serving; cannot be initiated after separation. Dependent children must wait until the member has 10+ years of service to start using it.
veteran_benefits.va_home_loan_note: VA-backed home loans generally require no down payment and no monthly PMI for veterans with full entitlement, subject to lender underwriting (credit/income/DTI) and a funding fee (1.25%-3.3% of loan amount depending on down payment and first/subsequent use, waived for veterans drawing service-connected disability compensation).
veteran_benefits.va_healthcare_priority_group_note: VA assigns one of 8 priority groups at enrollment based on service-connected disability rating, combat/toxic-exposure service history, income, and other VA-benefit receipt — not by specialty. A business-admin veteran's group assignment depends entirely on their individual disability rating and income profile, not their MOS/rating/AFSC.
veteran_benefits.state_benefit_variance_note: State veteran benefits (property-tax exemptions, income-tax treatment, tuition waivers, etc.) are set by individual state legislatures and are NOT a single national figure — e.g., a 100%-disabled veteran gets a full residence-homestead property-tax exemption in Texas but a flat $100,000 assessed-value exemption in California (data/veteran-benefits.json). Verify against the veteran's specific state of legal residence.

education_benefits.gi_bill_summary: Post-9/11 GI Bill pays full tuition/fees at public schools (in-state rate applies to all users) or up to $29,920.95/yr (2025-2026 AY) at private/foreign schools, plus a housing allowance keyed to the school's zip code and a $1,000/yr book stipend — scaled by the eligibility tier above. (data/education-benefits.json)
education_benefits.tuition_assistance: Search-sourced detail (UNVERIFIED at primary-source level, and possibly conflated with Coast Guard's Credentialing Assistance figure by the search summarizer per data/education-benefits.json) indicates the Coast Guard follows the same $250/semester-hour, $4,500/fiscal-year Tuition Assistance caps, administered under DHS via the Coast Guard Institute rather than DoD. Confirm at gocoastguard.com / uscg.mil before publishing.
education_benefits.credentialing_assistance: UNVERIFIED — inherited from data/education-benefits.json, where a search summary attributed a $4,500/year figure to the Coast Guard that appears to describe Tuition Assistance rather than Credentialing Assistance specifically (a likely conflation, not used here); a direct fetch to cool.osd.mil for the CA-specific figure was not re-attempted this session.
education_benefits.coop_or_apprenticeship_notes: SHRM-CP is confirmed listed on Coast Guard COOL (found via search this session, though under a leadership/petty-officer MOC code rather than a clearly YN-specific one) — NOT automatic from military service. Coast Guard-funded pathways to further education more broadly (as seen for the culinary cluster's CS rating in data/specialties-culinary.json) suggest a similar degree/certificate-funding posture may exist for YN, but this was not independently confirmed this session.

deployment_and_lifestyle.typical_tempo: Sea/shore rotation common to Coast Guard ratings — cutter-assigned YNs handle shipboard administration and pay support during patrols; shore-unit YNs (sector/district offices) are more garrison-stable. No YN-specific deployment-frequency figure was found this session.
deployment_and_lifestyle.family_considerations: Shore-unit assignments (sector/district administrative offices) are relatively family-stable; cutter assignments follow the Coast Guard's patrol-rotation pattern, which varies by cutter type and homeport.

bonuses.conditions: No current, verifiable enlistment or reenlistment bonus was found for any business-admin specialty in any branch this session. Administrative, HR, and finance/accounting specialties are historically LOW-bonus or NO-bonus fields across the services — they are high-accession, low-shortage, low-attrition career fields (unlike cyber, nuclear, or certain medical/aviation specialties that carry large shortage-driven bonuses), so the honest expectation for this cluster is $0 enlistment bonus in most cases. This session did not re-fetch the official HRC bonus chart (hrc.army.mil) or equivalent Navy/Air Force/Marine Corps/Coast Guard/Space Force bonus lists, consistent with this cluster's ~10-web-call budget being allocated primarily to entry requirements, training pipelines, and the credential-gap research the binding constraints prioritize. Bonuses are set quarterly per branch manning needs and change without notice — verify current bonus eligibility and amount directly with a recruiter before relying on any number. Space Force's overall accession volume is the smallest of the six branches, and administrative/finance functions are a shared, non-dedicated career field within it — the combination makes a Space-Force-specific bonus for this function especially unlikely, though this is a reasoned inference, not a sourced figure.
bonuses.source: No bonus-chart source fetched this session; general bonus-structure and quarterly-change pattern corroborated by data/specialties-culinary.json's bonus research (this project), which similarly found no verifiable culinary bonus and documented the same quarterly-change caveat.

retirement.twenty_year_cliff_note: The pension is all-or-nothing at 20 years — there is no partial/prorated defined-benefit pension for separating at 19 years or 10 years. Before 20 years, a member in this specialty keeps every dollar of their own TSP contributions and earnings (always vested), plus DoD's automatic 1%-of-pay TSP contribution and its match (vested after 2 years of service) — all portable into a civilian 401(k)/IRA. The defined-benefit pension itself is 'entirely forfeited' below 20 years, per DoD's own language. This applies identically regardless of specialty; business-admin carries no special rule.
retirement.continuation_pay_note: One-time cash payout available between roughly year 7 and year 12 of service (exact window and multiplier set annually per branch; Army's published active-component rate is 2.5x monthly basic pay for a 4-year additional service obligation). No business-admin-specific continuation-pay rate was identified this session — treat the general BRS figure as the baseline and verify current-branch amount before relying on it.
retirement.guard_reserve_points_note: Space Force has NO traditional Guard/Reserve component — it operates a single-component model in which Guardians serve either on 'sustained duty' (comparable to active duty) or 'not on sustained duty' status under the Space Force Personnel Management Act, structurally different from the points-based Guard/Reserve system described for the other five branches in this file. How retirement credit accrues for a Guardian in 'not on sustained duty' status was not independently researched this session — this is a structural fact flagged rather than an area where the standard Guard/Reserve boilerplate can be safely reused.
retirement.lump_sum_option_note: At 20+ years, a retiring member may elect 25% or 50% of the discounted present value of future retired pay as an immediate lump sum, in exchange for reduced monthly retired pay until full Social Security retirement age (~67), when the monthly amount returns to the uncut level. Fully taxable in the year received.

transition.skillbridge_notes: DoD-wide SkillBridge eligibility (up to 180 days of permissive duty in the final window before separation) is not specialty-restricted, so business-admin service members near separation are as eligible as any other MOS/rating/AFSC in principle — subject to the same real requirement as everyone else: unit commander approval, which is discretionary based on unit staffing/operational needs (data/transition.json). Administrative and finance skill sets are commonly matched with corporate HR-department, staffing-agency, or finance/accounting-firm SkillBridge industry partners in general SkillBridge program materials, but whether such partners are available in a given service member's location was not independently verified this session — flagged as an inference, not a sourced claim.
transition.tap_program_summary: Mandatory DoD-wide Transition Assistance Program: pre-separation counseling, a VA Benefits I course, a DOL employment-fundamentals workshop, and an optional 2-day track (Higher Education, Career/Technical Training, or Entrepreneurship/Boots to Business), ending in a Capstone that certifies Career Readiness Standards are met. Must begin no later than 365 days before separation. Applies identically regardless of specialty.
